title: cheilanthes microphylla var. aspidioides
intro: Cheilanthes microphylla var. aspidioides, also known as the rock fern or Aspidium fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Cheilanthes genus and is native to Australia and New Zealand. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties. Cheilanthes microphylla var. aspidioides can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, although its use should be cautioned and consulted with a healthcare professional before application.
